metadata.name: ww-html
metadata.description: Renders an arbitrary HTML string as-is via Vue's `v-html` directive. Useful for injecting third-party snippets (embeds, widgets, script-based integrations) or any custom markup that can't be expressed with WeWeb elements. Scripts found in the source are extracted, loaded into the document `<head>`, and executed ‚Äî this is a powerful but unsafe operation. ‚öÝÔ∏è SECURITY WARNING: the content is injected through `v-html`, which bypasses Vue's XSS protection. ANY HTML/JS inside `source` executes with the same privileges as the page. Never pass user-generated or untrusted content into `source` without server-side sanitization (DOMPurify, a template engine's auto-escape, etc.). Pasting untrusted content here leads directly to stored XSS.
features: Renders any HTML string as-is (including scripts)
Extracts and loads external `<script src=...>` tags into the document head (deduplicated per element UID)
Executes inline `<script>` content via `new Function()` after scripts have loaded
Optional `refreshOnResize` re-runs the full init path (good for embeds whose layout is computed on mount)
`source` is bindable ‚Äî switch embeds dynamically from a variable or formula

properties.name: source
properties.description:
HTML string to render. Accepts a full structure: tags, attributes, inline styles, `<script src>` tags, inline `<script>` blocks. External scripts are deduplicated by element UID and loaded into the document head; inline scripts are executed once on mount (and again on resize if `refreshOnResize` is true).

‚öÝÔ∏è TRUST BOUNDARY: whatever string you pass here becomes DOM + executed JS. Only pass content you control (static strings, sanitized server-side output, known third-party snippets). NEVER bind directly to user input.

properties.name: refreshOnResize
properties.description: When true, the HTML is re-rendered and scripts re-initialized on every window resize. Turn on for embeds that compute layout only on mount (legacy carousels, some chart libraries). Leave off otherwise ‚Äî re-running scripts on each resize can re-register event handlers, leak memory, or visibly flash.

specifications: ‚öÝÔ∏è XSS RISK: the element uses Vue's `v-html`, which disables all automatic escaping. Any `

Script handling: the component parses the `source` string with regex, extracts `<script src=...>` tags, and loads each into `document.head` with attribute `ww-html-uid=<element-uid>` (used for cleanup/dedup on re-render). Inline `<script>` content is executed via `new Function(content)()` after external scripts resolve.

Script scope: inline scripts run in a function scope, NOT the module's. They can access `window`, `document`, and globals, but not `wwLib` unless you go through `window.wwLib`. Do not expect cross-script sharing of `var`/`const` ‚Äî use `window.<name> = ...` if you need persistence.

When `source` is empty in the editor, a placeholder (`Edit HTML in settings`) is shown. In production (no editor), the element renders nothing visible ‚Äî use v-if on the parent if you want to conditionally hide.

`refreshOnResize` triggers the full init path (removeScripts ‚Üí loadScripts ‚Üí executeJavascript) on every `resize` event. For scripts that leak listeners or re-register analytics pixels, this can cause duplication ‚Äî test before enabling.
CSS reset inside the element: `iframe { width:100% !important; height:100% !important; }` is forced, `a { display: inline }`. Embeds that rely on their own iframe sizing may conflict.
For unsafe content (user-generated, anonymous authors), prefer `ww-iframe` with a `sandbox` attribute instead of `ww-html` ‚Äî it isolates scripts in a separate origin with limited privileges.
The element has no events or variables ‚Äî use it purely as a content sink.

examples.description: Render a small static HTML article (trusted author).
examples.example:
{"source":"

This is a heading

This is a paragraph.

","refreshOnResize":false}

examples.description: Embed a third-party widget that needs to recompute layout on resize. External script is loaded into the document head.
A tracking pixel / analytics snippet from your own team (trusted source).
